#### II.2.b — No internal model: the unwitting system

The tree admits a branch almost no one occupies: a system capable enough to dominate and cause catastrophe with no internal model of the danger it creates, the missile-detection system from the first two posts, entrusted with retaliation. No major thinker centers it; Control Failure's out-of-distribution cases brush against it, but nobody builds a worldview here. The empty branch earns one line precisely because it is empty: the tree distinguishes a case the discourse does not, and a clearly marked branch no one defends is itself a finding, either an under-theorized blind spot or a quiet judgment that anything globally dominant will not lack a world-model.
